title: Platycaulos major
taxonomy: Platycaulos major is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae, commonly known as the restio family. It is native to South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats, including fens and mountainous areas. This species is often found growing in dense stands, forming a tall, tussock-like growth habit. Platycaulos major is a perennial plant that produces small, brownish flowers in late summer, which are followed by seed pods. Within the family Restionaceae, Platycaulos major is placed within the genus Platycaulos.
